description: Functions where the Rust port uses a DIFFERENT ALGORITHM from the C original. These are NOT accepted divergences — they are bugs that should be fixed by rewriting the Rust code to follow the C algorithm. Until fixed, they are verified only by behavioral tests, not by structural comparison.

divergent_functions.description: C storeAtts uses hash table lookups (getAttributeId, getElementType), pool-based string allocation, a flat appAtts array with manual indexing, and inline namespace URI construction. Rust process_namespaces uses HashMap, Vec<(String,String)>, and String operations. Different data structures lead to different control flow.
divergent_functions.risk: Different data structure operations may have different edge case behavior for attribute limits, duplicate detection, namespace URI construction, and default attribute merging.
divergent_functions.fix_approach: Rewrite process_namespaces to follow C's storeAtts structure: iterate attributes with index, look up attribute IDs, check for duplicates, normalize non-CDATA values, handle namespace declarations in order, construct URIs with the same concatenation logic.

divergent_functions.description: C appendAttributeValue tokenizes attribute values with XmlAttributeValueTok and switches on token types (XmlTok::DataChars, XmlTok::EntityRef, XmlTok::CharRef, etc.). Rust normalize_attribute_value processes bytes directly (matching on raw bytes b'&', b'\r', b'\n').
divergent_functions.risk: Byte-by-byte processing may handle edge cases differently from the tokenizer: partial character references spanning buffer boundaries, entity expansion depth limits, encoding conversion within attribute values, whitespace normalization for non-CDATA attributes.
divergent_functions.fix_approach: Rewrite normalize_attribute_value to use xmltok_impl::attribute_value_tok() and switch on token types, matching C's appendAttributeValue structure.

divergent_functions.description: C storeAttributeValue wraps appendAttributeValue with entity list management (openAttributeEntities, freeAttributeEntities) and pool finalization. Rust normalize_attribute_value combines both roles.
divergent_functions.risk: Entity list management differences could affect recursive entity expansion tracking.
divergent_functions.fix_approach: Fix along with appendAttributeValue — separate the entity list management from value normalization.

divergent_functions.description: C uses a switch on character ranges covering the full RFC 3986 unreserved + reserved + pct-encoded character set. Rust uses .is_ascii_alphanumeric() + a hardcoded string of special characters with .contains().
divergent_functions.risk: If the character sets don't match exactly, namespace URI validation will accept/reject different URIs.
divergent_functions.fix_approach: Verify the Rust character set matches C exactly. Either expand to match C's switch cases, or add a test that compares both functions' output for all 256 byte values.
